name: Remove-PSSession
-ComputerName
-Cn
options.description: Specifies an array of names of computers. This cmdlet closes the PSSessions that are connected to the specified computers. Wildcard characters are permitted.

options.name: -ContainerId
options.description: Specifies an array of IDs of containers. This cmdlet removes sessions for each of the specified containers. Use the `docker ps` command to get a list of container IDs. For more information, see the help for the docker ps (https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/ps/)command.

options.name: -Id
options.description: Specifies an array of IDs of sessions. This cmdlet closes the PSSessions with the specified IDs. Type one or more IDs, separated by commas, or use the range operator (`..`) to specify a range of IDs.

options.name: -InstanceId
options.description: Specifies an array of instance IDs. This cmdlet closes the PSSessions that have the specified instance IDs.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies an array of friendly names of sessions. This cmdlet closes the PSSessions that have the specified friendly names. Wildcard characters are permitted.

options.name: -Session
options.description: Specifies the session objects of the PSSessions to close. Enter a variable that contains the PSSessions or a command that creates or gets the PSSessions , such as a `New-PSSession` or `Get-PSSession` command. You can also pipe one or more session objects to `Remove-PSSession`.

name: -VMId
-VMGuid
options.description: Specifies an array of ID of virtual machines. This cmdlet starts an interactive session with each of the specified virtual machines. To see the virtual machines that are available to you, use the following command:

options.name: -VMName
options.description: Specifies an array of names of virtual machines. This cmdlet starts an interactive session with each of the specified virtual machines. To see the virtual machines that are available to you, use the `Get-VM` cmdlet.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

description: Closes one or more PowerShell sessions (PSSessions).
